title: Controlling the 3D view
text: Three angles control plot oriented: pitch, roll, yaw rotate plot around x-, y-, z-axes, respectively. specified degrees. ’re zero, plot resembles standard 2D plot (+x = right, +y = , +z = toward viewer). defaults (pitch = 0, roll = -60, yaw = -30) give overhead corner view can work well many plots. variety rotations showing default, zero-rotation case, effect setting rotation angle individually others held zero, example combining achieve arbitrary rotation (note uses patchwork library produce mutlti-panel plot):
code: (p + coord_3d() + ggtitle("Default")) +   (p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 0, yaw = 0) + ggtitle("All angles 0")) +   (p + coord_3d(pitch = 20, roll = 40, yaw = 60) + ggtitle("Arbitrary combination")) +   (p + coord_3d(pitch = 30, roll = 0, yaw = 0) + ggtitle("pitch = 30")) +   (p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 30, yaw = 0) + ggtitle("roll = 30")) +   (p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 0, yaw = 30) + ggtitle("yaw = 30")) +   plot_layout(nrow = 2)

title: Controlling the 3D view
text: default, coord_3d() uses perspective projection, objects farther viewer appear smaller. dist parameter controls camera distance center data cube — larger values produce less distortion. Set persp = FALSE switch orthographic projection, parallel lines 3D remain parallel 2D rendering. Orthographic projection equivalent viewing infinite distance can useful technical schematic plots.
code: (p + coord_3d(yaw = 45, dist = 1.1) + ggtitle("dist = 1.1")) +   (p + coord_3d(yaw = 45, dist = 2) + ggtitle("dist = 2")) +   (p + coord_3d(yaw = 45, persp = FALSE) + ggtitle("persp = FALSE")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 3)

title: Controlling the 3D view
text: scales parameter controls data ranges map visual cube dimensions. "free" (default), axis stretched independently fill dimension cube, maximizing visual range variable. "fixed", visual proportions match data proportions, analogous coord_fixed() 2D:  ratio parameter sets custom relative lengths three axes. "free" scales, ratios applied standardized cube coordinates. "fixed" scales, ’re applied data coordinates:
code: (p + coord_3d(scales = "free") + ggtitle('scales = "free"')) +   (p + coord_3d(scales = "fixed") + ggtitle('scales = "fixed"')) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2) & theme_light() (p + coord_3d(scales = "free", ratio = c(1, 3, 1)) +     ggtitle('free, ratio = c(1, 3, 1)')) +   (p + coord_3d(scales = "fixed", ratio = c(1, 3, 1)) +       ggtitle('fixed, ratio = c(1, 3, 1)')) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2) & theme_light()

title: Controlling the 3D view
text: panels argument controls faces cube drawn. six faces named "xmin", "xmax", "ymin", "ymax", "zmin", "zmax". Depending rotation, faces lie behind data (“background” panels) lie front (“foreground” panels). default, background panels drawn. drawn, foreground panels default 20% opacity don’t obscure data; styling controlled via theme elements (see theming section ). can request specific faces name, show faces, hide entirely:
code: (p + coord_3d(panels = "background") + ggtitle('"background" (default)')) +   (p + coord_3d(panels = c("xmin", "xmax", "zmax")) +       ggtitle('c("xmin", "xmax", "zmax")')) +   (p + coord_3d(panels = "all") + ggtitle('"all"')) +   (p + coord_3d(panels = "none") + ggtitle('"none"')) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2)

title: Controlling the 3D view
text: zoom adjusts overall framing without changing rotation projection. Values less 1 zoom (whitespace), values greater 1 zoom (tighter framing). clip parameter controls whether graphical elements can drawn outside panel area (ggplot2’s rectangular canvas, projected 3D panel). default "" allows , generally recommended 3D plots elements may extend beyond projected panel area. expand parameter works like expand argument standard ggplot2 scales — TRUE (default), axis ranges padded slightly beyond data range. can fine-tune expansion per axis using standard scale functions.
code: (p + coord_3d(zoom = 0.7) + ggtitle("zoom = 0.7")) +   (p + coord_3d(zoom = 1) + ggtitle("zoom = 1\n(default)")) +   (p + coord_3d(zoom = 1.5, clip = "on") + ggtitle('zoom = 1.5\nclip = "on"')) +   plot_layout(ncol = 3) (p + coord_3d(expand = TRUE) + ggtitle("expand = TRUE (default)")) +   (p + coord_3d(expand = FALSE) + ggtitle("expand = FALSE")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2)

title: Controlling the 3D view
text: xlabels, ylabels, zlabels parameters control axis tick labels titles placed. axis potentially multiple possible edges labels appear (depending faces visible). default "auto" uses heuristic prioritizes edges periphery plot readability. manual control, pass length-2 character vector naming two adjacent faces — shared edge labels go. first face determines alignment plane:  rotate_labels parameter controls whether labels automatically rotate align projected axis direction. Setting FALSE uses angle specified theme settings instead:  title_position parameter affects axis titles fall internal (non-peripheral) edges. "auto" places near end axis outside plot area, "center" centers along edge.
code: (p + coord_3d() + ggtitle("auto (default)")) +   (p + coord_3d(xlabels = c("ymax", "zmax"),                 zlabels = c("xmax", "ymin")) +       ggtitle("manual placement")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2) & theme_light() (p + coord_3d(rotate_labels = TRUE) + ggtitle("rotate_labels = TRUE")) +   (p + coord_3d(rotate_labels = FALSE) + ggtitle("rotate_labels = FALSE")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2) & theme_light()

title: Controlling the 3D view
text: Standard ggplot2 themes work ggcube. Adding complete theme behaves expected:  ggcube also adds several theme elements 3D-specific styling. One important category foreground panel elements, control cube faces rendered front data. inherit background counterparts, styling panel.background affects background foreground faces, panel.foreground targets front faces. ggcube’s element_rect() extends ggplot2’s version alpha parameter transparency. particularly useful foreground panels — default panel.foreground uses alpha = 0.2 front faces don’t obscure data:  axis labels, ggcube adds axis.text.z axis.title.z elements inherit axis.text axis.title respectively. margin argument element_text() controls padding titles, labels, cube edges. ggcube’s current system text spacing fairly crude, margins need manually adjusted often base ggplot2 figures.  full set 3D-specific theme elements: panel.foreground: Fill border foreground cube faces (inherits panel.background) panel.border.foreground: Border foreground faces (inherits panel.border) panel.grid.foreground: Grid lines foreground faces (inherits panel.grid) panel.grid.major.foreground: Major grid lines foreground faces (inherits panel.grid.foreground) axis.text.z: Z-axis tick labels (inherits axis.text) axis.title.z: Z-axis title (inherits axis.title)
code: (p + coord_3d() + theme_dark() + ggtitle("theme_dark()")) +   (p + coord_3d() + theme_minimal() + ggtitle("theme_minimal()")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2) p +   coord_3d(panels = "all") +   theme_gray() +   theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black"),         panel.foreground = element_rect(alpha = .3),         panel.grid.foreground = element_line(color = "gray", linewidth = .25)) p +   coord_3d(panels = "all") +   theme(panel.background = element_rect(color = "black"),         panel.border = element_rect(color = "black"),         panel.foreground = element_rect(alpha = .3),         panel.grid.foreground = element_line(color = "gray", linewidth = .25),         axis.text = element_text(color = "darkblue", size = 12),         axis.text.z = element_text(color = "darkred"),         axis.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 30)),         axis.title.x = element_text(color = "magenta"))

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: essential ingredient ggcube plot coord_3d(). Adding standard ggplot includes z aesthetic creates 3D plot:  standard ggplot2 layers like geom_point() work 3D automatically. ggcube also provides 3D-specific layer functions surfaces, paths, volumes, text, described . ggcube works within ggplot2’s 2D graphics engine, important things understand rendering works. 3D layer projects geometry onto 2D plane, depth sorting determine elements appear front others. sorting happens within layer, across layers — just standard ggplot2, later layers drawn top earlier ones regardless 3D depth. means layer order code matters, complex multi-layer scenes may require thought stacking.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy, z = drv, color = class)) +       geom_point() +       coord_3d()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: coord_3d() controls 3D scene projected onto 2D. Rotation specified via three angles — pitch (tilt around x-axis), roll (tilt around y-axis), yaw (spin around z-axis):  Perspective projection default, making distant objects appear smaller. dist parameter controls strength effect (larger values = less distortion), persp = FALSE switches orthographic projection parallel lines stay parallel. scales parameter controls axis ranges map visual size. "free" (default) stretches axis independently fill cube, "fixed" preserves relative scale data (like coord_fixed() 2D). ratio parameter lets set custom proportions three axes. zoom adjusts overall framing without changing rotation projection. See 3D view article comprehensive guide view parameters.
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, drv, color = class)) +       geom_point() +       coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 60, yaw = 0, dist = 1.4,                ratio = c(2, 1, 1), panels = "all") +       theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black"),             panel.foreground = element_rect(alpha = .1))

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: standard ggplot2 geoms designed around 2D coordinate assumptions, won’t render correctly coord_3d(). (exception geom_point(), works box shown , albeit ordering sizing limitations.) ggcube provides range 3D-native layer functions cover common 3D plot types, including points, surfaces, bars, paths, text. ’s quick tour main categories.

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: Several geoms stats work together render surfaces. geom_surface_3d() renders data tessellated surface, geom_contour_3d() creates layer-cake contour stacks, geom_ridgeline_3d() shows cross-sectional slices:  Stats like stat_function_3d(), stat_smooth_3d(), stat_density_3d() generate surface data functions, model fits, kernel density estimates. stats can paired surface geoms. See 3D surfaces article detail surface plotting options.
code: ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_surface_3d(aes(fill = z, color = z)) +       scale_fill_viridis_c() + scale_color_viridis_c() +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1.5, 2, 1), expand = FALSE, panels = "zmin",                light = light(direction = c(1, 0, 0))) +       guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +       theme_light()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: geom_point_3d() extends scatter plots depth-scaled point sizes (closer points shown larger), proper depth sorting (closer points plotted top), optional reference lines points project onto cube faces:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy, z = drv, fill = class)) +       geom_point_3d(size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = .1,                     ref_lines = TRUE, ref_faces = c("ymax", "xmax")) +       coord_3d()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: geom_path_3d() connects observations depth-sorted, depth-scaled line segments:  geom_segment_3d() also available drawing individual segments defined start end coordinates.
code: x <- seq(0, 20*pi, pi/16) spiral <- data.frame(x = x, y = sin(x), z = cos(x), time = 1:length(x)) ggplot(spiral, aes(x, y, z, color = time)) +       geom_path_3d() +       scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("blue", "purple", "red", "orange")) +       coord_3d() +       theme_light()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: geom_col_3d() produces 3D column charts, geom_bar_3d() creates 3D histograms automatic binning, geom_voxel_3d() renders arrays cubes:
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Species, Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +       geom_bar_3d(bins = 20, width = c(.5, 1)) +       coord_3d(scales = "fixed", ratio = c(1, 3, .25), yaw = 60) +       scale_z_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) +       theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: geom_hull_3d() computes renders triangulated hulls 3D point clouds, including convex alpha hulls:
code: ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_hull_3d(method = "convex", fill = "#9e2602", color = "#5e1600") +       coord_3d()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: stat_distributions_3d() computes 1D kernel density estimates per group arranges ridgeline surfaces, 3D analog ggridges::geom_density_ridges():
code: ggplot(iris, aes(y = Sepal.Length, x = Species, fill = Species)) +       stat_distributions_3d() +       scale_z_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, NA))) +       coord_3d() +       theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: geom_text_3d() renders text 3D, either “billboard” labels always face camera, polygon outlines can oriented direction:
code: df <- expand.grid(x = c("H", "B"), y = c("a", "o", "u"), z = c("g", "t")) df$label <- paste0(df$x, df$y, df$z) ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z, label = label, fill = x)) +       geom_text_3d(method = "polygon", facing = "zmax",                    size = 5, weight = "bold") +       coord_3d(scales = "fixed", light = NULL)

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: Lighting modifies fill /color polygon faces based orientation relative light source, giving surfaces sense depth shape. ’s controlled via light() function, can passed coord_3d() (applying layers) individual layer functions (overriding coord-level setting):  Use light = "none" disable lighting entirely, light = NULL layer inherit coord-level setting. comprehensive guide lighting methods, color modes, light direction, backface handling, see lighting article.
code: p <- ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_hull_3d(fill = "#9e2602", color = "#5e1600")  p + coord_3d(light = light(method = "direct", mode = "hsl",                            direction = c(0, 0, 1)))

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: ggcube provides scale_z_continuous() scale_z_discrete() controlling z-axis, interface x/y counterparts. zlim() shorthand setting z-axis limits:
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, wt, z = qsec)) +       geom_point() +       zlim(15, 20) +       coord_3d()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: lighting active, standard color guides don’t reflect shading visible plot. guide_colorbar_3d() guide_legend_3d() create guides show range shaded colors:
code: ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z, fill = z)) +       stat_surface_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", direction = c(1, 0, 0))) +       guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +       scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("tomato", "dodgerblue")) +       coord_3d()

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: panels argument coord_3d() controls cube faces drawn. Faces behind data “background” panels; faces front “foreground” panels (default semi-transparent don’t obscure data): Standard ggplot2 themes theme() customization work expected. ggcube adds foreground-specific elements (panel.foreground, panel.grid.foreground, panel.border.foreground) z-axis text elements (axis.text.z, axis.title.z). ggcube’s element_rect() extends ggplot2’s version alpha parameter transparency. details, see theming section 3D coordinates article.
code: ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_hull_3d() +       coord_3d(panels = "all") +       theme(panel.background = element_rect(color = "black"),             panel.border = element_rect(color = "black"),             panel.foreground = element_rect(alpha = .3),             panel.grid.foreground = element_line(color = "gray", linewidth = .25),             axis.text = element_text(color = "darkblue"),             axis.text.z = element_text(color = "darkred"),             axis.title = element_text(margin = margin(t = 30)),             axis.title.x = element_text(color = "magenta"))

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: annotate_3d() adds reference geometry — points, text labels, segments — 3D layer. Unlike adding separate layer, annotations embedded within host layer participate depth sorting:
code: summit <- filter(mountain, z == max(z)) ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_contour_3d(             annotate = list(                   annotate_3d("point", x = summit$x, y = summit$y, z = summit$z, color = "red"),                   annotate_3d("text", x = summit$x, y = summit$y, z = summit$z, color = "red",                               label = "Summit", fontface = "bold", vjust = -1)             ), fill = "black"       ) +       coord_3d(ratio = c(2, 3, 1.5), light = "none")

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: position_on_face() lets project layers onto cube faces, enabling mix 3D 2D content. can flatten 3D layer onto face, place natively 2D layer (like stat_density_2d()) onto specific face using axes parameter:
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, Petal.Length,                  color = Species, fill = Species)) +       coord_3d() + xlim(4, 8) +       stat_density_2d(position = position_on_face(faces = "zmin", axes = c("x", "y")),                       geom = "polygon", alpha = .1, linewidth = .25) +       geom_hull_3d(position = position_on_face("ymax"), alpha = .5) +       geom_point_3d(shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = .25)

title: Introduction to ggcube
text: animate_3d() creates animated rotations ggcube plot. Rotation angles specified keyframe vectors get interpolated across frames: Output format controlled via renderers: gifski_renderer_3d() (GIF, default), av_renderer_3d() (MP4), file_renderer_3d() (individual frames). Use anim_save_3d() save result file.
code: p <- ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_contour_3d(fill = "black", color = "white", linewidth = .5) +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1.5, 2, 1), light = "none", zoom = 1.5) +       theme_void()  animate_3d(p, yaw = c(0, 360))

title: Lighting and shading
text: Lighting can set two levels: Coord-level: coord_3d(light = light(...)) applies layers plot. Layer-level: geom_*_3d(light = light(...)) overrides coord-level setting layer. Use light = "none" disable lighting entirely, light = NULL layer inherit coord-level setting (default layer behavior). add second layer plot, override inherited light settings disable lighting layer:  fill color parameters control aesthetics get modified lighting. default TRUE:  Lighting works solid aesthetically-mapped colors:
code: p + geom_hull_3d(aes(x = x + 2.5), fill = "#9e2602", color = "#5e1600",                  light = "none") (p + ggtitle("fill + color (default)")) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light(fill = TRUE, color = FALSE)) +       ggtitle("fill only")) ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_hull_3d(aes(fill = x, color = x)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   scale_color_viridis_c() +   guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +   coord_3d(light = light("direct"))

title: Lighting and shading
text: method parameter controls lighting model: "diffuse" (default): Soft, atmospheric lighting. surfaces pointing directly away light source fully dark. "direct": Hard lighting. surfaces angled beyond 90 degrees light fully dark. "rgb": Maps surface orientation RGB color space, replacing base colors entirely.
code: (p + coord_3d(light = light(method = "diffuse")) + ggtitle('"diffuse" (default)')) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light(method = "direct")) + ggtitle('"direct"')) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light(method = "rgb")) + ggtitle('"rgb"')) +   plot_layout(ncol = 3)

title: Lighting and shading
text: mode parameter controls lighting modifies colors. applies "diffuse" "direct" methods ("rgb"). two color modes: "hsv" (default): Modifies value component HSV color. Highlights preserve hue; shadows fade black. "hsl": Modifies lightness component HSL color. Highlights fade white; shadows fade black. two modes give identical results grayscale base colors.
code: (p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsv")) +     ggtitle('"hsv" (default)')) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl")) +       ggtitle('"hsl"'))

title: Lighting and shading
text: contrast parameter scales intensity lighting effects. Values less 1 give subtler effects; values greater 1 give dramatic ones:
code: (p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", contrast = 0.5)) +     ggtitle("contrast = 0.5")) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", contrast = 1)) +       ggtitle("contrast = 1 (default)")) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", contrast = 1.5)) +       ggtitle("contrast = 1.5")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 3)

title: Lighting and shading
text: direction parameter length-3 vector specifying light comes ; example, direction = c(0, -1, 0) lights plot negative y direction. anchor parameter controls whether direction relative data axes camera: anchor = "scene" (default): Direction relative data axes rotates plot. direction = c(1, 0, 0) always lights surfaces facing “xmax” face. anchor = "camera": Direction fixed relative viewing pane. direction = c(1, 0, 0) always lights surfaces facing right side plot, regardless rotation.
code: (p + coord_3d(light = light("direct", direction = c(0, -1, 0))) +     ggtitle('anchor = "scene"')) +   (p + coord_3d(light = light("direct", direction = c(0, -1, 0), anchor = "camera")) +       ggtitle('anchor = "camera"'))

title: Lighting and shading
text: addition directional lighting (parallel rays direction), ggcube also supports positional lighting point source. Use position instead direction specify light source location data coordinates. Setting distance_falloff = TRUE applies inverse-square intensity falloff light source position.
code: ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +   stat_surface_3d(fill = "red", color = "red") +   coord_3d(     light = light(position = c(.5, .7, 95), distance_falloff = TRUE,                   mode = "hsl", contrast = .9),     ratio = c(1.5, 2, 1))

title: Lighting and shading
text: backface side polygon facing underside surface inside volume. default, backfaces receive inverted lighting relative frontfaces (backface_scale = -1), creating strong visual contrast two sides. Setting backface_scale = 1 lights backfaces frontfaces. Modifying backface_offset uniformly darkens brightens backfaces.
code:
pb <- ggplot() +   geom_function_3d(fun = function(x, y) x^2 + y^2,                    xlim = c(-3, 3), ylim = c(-3, 3),                    fill = "steelblue", color = "steelblue")  (pb + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -70, yaw = 0,                light = light(mode = "hsl")) +     ggtitle("default:
backface_scale = -1
backface_offset = 0")) +   (pb + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -70, yaw = 0,                  light = light(backface_scale = 1, mode = "hsl")) +       ggtitle("
backface_scale = 1
backface_offset = 0")) +   (pb + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -70, yaw = 0,               light = light(backface_scale = 1, mode = "hsl",                             backface_offset = -.5)) +       ggtitle("
backface_scale = 1
backface_offset = -0.5")) +   plot_layout(nrow = 1)

title: Lighting and shading
text: lighting active, standard color guides don’t reflect range shaded colors visible plot. ggcube provides guide_colorbar_3d() guide_legend_3d() create guides show full shading gradient:   guides accept shade_range control range shading shown (length-2 vector range -1 1), reverse_shade flip gradient direction. Note fill color map variable, ggplot2 creates shared scale. case, apply 3D guide via guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()), via color guide.
code: ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z, fill = z)) +   stat_surface_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", direction = c(1, 0, 0))) +   guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +   scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("tomato", "dodgerblue")) +   coord_3d(ratio = c(2, 3, 1.5)) ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z, fill = x > .5, group = 1)) +   stat_surface_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", direction = c(1, 0, 0))) +   guides(fill = guide_legend_3d()) +   coord_3d(ratio = c(2, 3, 1.5))

title: Surfaces
text: Surface data comes two forms: Regular grids z value every combination x y positions — like raster DEM. Grid data can supplied directly user, generated internally stat. Irregular points scattered (x, y, z) observations grid structure. triangulated via Delaunay tessellation produce surface mesh. stat_surface_3d() auto-detects type data receives handles . three surface geoms work regular grid data, geom_surface_3d() supports irregular points (since ridgelines contours require underlying grid structure).

title: Surfaces
text: Three geoms render surface data different visual styles.

title: Surfaces
text: geom_surface_3d() primary surface geom. can use create solid surface shown , set fill = NA produce wireframe. tessellates data polygon tiles supports regular grids irregular point data:
code: p <- ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z, fill = z, color = z)) +   coord_3d(ratio = c(1, 1.5, .75)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   scale_color_viridis_c() +   theme_light()  p + geom_surface_3d(light = light(direction = c(1, 0, .5),                                 mode = "hsv", contrast = 1.5),                   linewidth = .2) +   guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d())

title: Surfaces
text: geom_contour_3d() creates filled contour bands stacked respective elevations, like topographic layer-cake. bins breaks parameters control contour levels:
code: p + geom_contour_3d(bins = 12, color = "white", light = "none")

title: Surfaces
text: geom_ridgeline_3d() slices surface cross-sections, producing ridgeline view. direction parameter controls slicing axis:
code: p + stat_surface_3d(geom = "ridgeline_3d", direction = "y",                     fill = "black", color = "white",                     light = "none", linewidth = .1)

title: Surfaces
text: geoms can paired surface stats described . example, stat_function_3d(geom = "contour_3d") renders mathematical function contour bands, stat_function_3d(geom = "ridgeline_3d") renders ridgelines.

title: Surfaces
text: Four stats produce surface data. generates regular grid (x, y, z) points can rendered three surface geoms.

title: Surfaces
text: stat_surface_3d() default stat geom_surface_3d(). works regular grids, irregular point data performs Delaunay triangulation shown :
code: set.seed(42) pts <- data.frame(x = runif(200, -2, 2), y = runif(200, -2, 2)) pts$z <- with(pts, sin(x) * cos(y))  ggplot(pts, aes(x, y, z = z, fill = z)) +   stat_surface_3d(sort_method = "pairwise") +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   coord_3d(light = "none")

title: Surfaces
text: stat_function_3d() evaluates function f(x, y) grid:
code: ggplot(mapping = aes(color = after_stat(x))) +   geom_function_3d(fun = function(x, y) sin(x) * cos(y),                    xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-pi, pi),                    fill = NA) + # disable fill to make wireframe   coord_3d(light = "none")

title: Surfaces
text: stat_smooth_3d() (geom_smooth_3d()) fits model scattered (x, y, z) data renders fitted surface. options different model types, overlaying data point residual lines, adding confidence interval surfaces shown :
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(qsec, disp, mpg, fill = after_stat(level))) +       geom_smooth_3d(domain = "chull", se = TRUE, color = "black") +       scale_fill_manual(values = c("red", "darkorchid4", "steelblue")) +       coord_3d(yaw = 150) + theme_light()

title: Surfaces
text: stat_density_3d() (geom_density_3d()) computes 2D kernel density estimate. options modifying bandwidth, resolution:
code: ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   geom_density_3d(min_ndensity = .01) +   guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +   coord_3d() +   scale_fill_viridis_c()

title: Surfaces
text: following options apply geom_surface_3d() tessellated mesh produces.

title: Surfaces
text: grid parameter controls tile geometry. default "rectangle" produces rectangular tiles. "right1" "right2" split rectangle right triangles along opposite diagonals, "equilateral" produces equilateral triangular lattice. Triangulated grids can prevent lighting artifacts sharply curving surfaces:
code: d <- dplyr::mutate(tidyr::expand_grid(x = -10:10, y = -10:10),                    z = sqrt(x^2 + y^2) / 1.5,                    z = cos(z) - z) p <- ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", direction = c(1, 0, 0)))  (p + geom_surface_3d(fill = "white", color = "darkblue",                       linewidth = .2) +     ggtitle('"rectangle" (default)')) +   (p + geom_surface_3d(fill = "white", color = "darkblue",                         linewidth = .2, grid = "right1") +       ggtitle('"right1"')) +   (p + geom_surface_3d(fill = "white", color = "darkblue",                         linewidth = .2, grid = "right2") +       ggtitle('"right2"')) +   (p + geom_surface_3d(fill = "white", color = "darkblue",                         linewidth = .2, grid = "equilateral") +       ggtitle('"equilateral"')) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2)

title: Surfaces
text: Surface stats compute gradient information grid point, available via after_stat(): partial derivatives (dzdx, dzdy), gradient magnitude (slope), direction steepest ascent (aspect):
code: p + geom_surface_3d(aes(fill = after_stat(slope)), grid = "right2") +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d())

title: Surfaces
text: Surfaces often benefit ggcube’s lighting system, modifies polygon face colors based orientation relative light source. Note since contours ridgelines uniform polygon orientation, typically benefit lighting. See lighting article details.
code: p <- ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +   coord_3d(ratio = c(1, 1.5, .75)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() + scale_color_viridis_c() +   theme(legend.position = "none")  (p + geom_surface_3d(aes(fill = z, color = z), light = "none") +     ggtitle('light = "none"')) +   (p + geom_surface_3d(aes(fill = z, color = z),                         light = light(direction = c(1, 0, 0))) +       ggtitle("diffuse (default)")) +   plot_layout(ncol = 2)

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: ggcube lets build 3D figures using ggplot2. Use create 3D surfaces, volumes, scatter plots, complex layered visualizations using familiar ggplot2 syntax aes(x, y, z) coord_3d(). package provides variety 3D-specific geoms render surfaces, prisms, points, paths, text 3D; also works standard ggplot2 layer functions. can control plot geometry 3D projection parameters, apply range 3D lighting models, mix 3D layers 2D layers rendered cube faces. Standard ggplot2 features like faceting, themes, scales, legends work expected. 3D plots wonderful exploration, storytelling, data art. note precise quantitative communication, occlusion perspective distortion can problematic, 2D usually better choice.

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
code: # You can install the package from CRAN: install.packages("ggcube")  # Or get the development version from GitHub: devtools::install_github("matthewkling/ggcube")

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: R several tools 3D visualization. ggcube designed users want stay within ggplot2 ecosystem. packages offer different tradeoffs: plotly rgl provide interactive 3D viewers rotation zoom via WebGL/OpenGL, using APIs. rayshader produces ray-traced renderings terrain data photorealistic lighting shadows; also makes 3D popups ggplot2 figures, 3D ggplots per se. gg3D another ggplot2 extension 3D plotting, longer actively maintained.

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: essential ingredient ggcube plot coord_3d(). Adding standard ggplot, providing z aesthetic variable, creates 3D plot:  can control plot rotation, perspective, dimensions, well axis label placement panel selection, via parameters coord_3d(). See 3D view article comprehensive guide.
code: library(ggplot2) library(ggcube)  # Basic 3D scatter plot ggplot(mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy, z = drv, color = class)) +       geom_point() +       coord_3d() ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, drv, color = class)) +       geom_point() +       coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 60, yaw = 0, dist = 1.4,                 ratio = c(2, 1, 1), panels = "all") +       theme(panel.border = element_rect(color = "black"),             panel.foreground = element_rect(alpha = .1))

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: geom_surface_3d() renders surfaces based existing grid data terrain data geom_ridgeline_3d() renders surfaces series cross-sections geom_contour_3d() renders surfaces layer cakes stacked contours stat_function() visualizes mathematical functions stat_smooth_3d() fits statistical models two predictors visualizes fitted surfaces confidence intervals stat_density_3d() creates perspective visualizations 2D kernel density estimates stat_hull_3d() plots triangulated volumes based convex alpha hulls 3D points See surfaces article full guide surface system. Example: terrain surface using geom_surface_3d():  Example: terrain surface using geom_contour_3d(), also showing functionality animation rotating GIF:  Example: mathematical surface using geom_function_3d(): Example: fitted model surface using geom_smooth_3d():
code: ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_surface_3d(aes(fill = z, color = z)) +       scale_fill_viridis_c() + scale_color_viridis_c() +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1.5, 2, 1), expand = FALSE, panels = "zmin",                light = light(direction = c(1, 0, 0))) +       guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +       theme_light() p <- ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +       geom_contour_3d(fill = "black", color = "white", linewidth = .5) +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1.5, 2, 1), light = "none") +       theme_void() animate_3d(p, yaw = c(0, 360)) ggplot() +       geom_function_3d(fun = function(x1, x2) cos(x1) * sin(x2),                        xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-2*pi, 2*pi),                        fill = "#7a2100", color = "#b3725b",                         grid = "right1", linewidth = .2) +       coord_3d(yaw = 160, roll = -70,                 scales = "fixed", ratio = c(1, 1, 2)) +       labs(x = expression(x[1]),            y = expression(x[2]),            z = expression(cos(x[1]) %*% sin(x[2]))) +       theme_minimal() # Generate scattered 3D data set.seed(123) d <- data.frame(x = rnorm(50),                 y = rnorm(50)) d$z <- d$x + d$x^2 - d$y^2 + rnorm(50)  # Plot GAM fit with uncertainty layers ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +        geom_smooth_3d(aes(fill = after_stat(level)),                      method = "gam", formula = z ~ te(x, y),                      se = TRUE, level = 0.99,                      color = "black", grid = "equilateral") +       scale_fill_manual(values = c("red", "darkorchid4", "steelblue")) +       coord_3d(light = NULL)

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: geom_path_3d() renders paths 3D space depth-based sorting scaling:
code: butterfly <- ggcube:::lorenz_attractor(n_points = 8000, dt = .01) ggplot(butterfly, aes(x, y, z, color = time)) +       geom_path_3d(linewidth = 0.1, color = "black",                    position = position_on_face(c("xmax", "ymax", "zmin"))) +       geom_path_3d(linewidth = 0.3) +       scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("blue", "purple", "red", "orange")) +       coord_3d() +       theme_light()

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: ggplot2::geom_point() works ggcube demonstrated , geom_point_3d() creates 3D-aware scatter plots proper point ordering, depth-scaled point sizes, options include reference lines reference points projecting 3D points onto 2D face panels:
code: ggplot(mpg, aes(x = displ, y = hwy, z = drv, fill = class)) +       geom_point_3d(size = 3, shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = .1,                     ref_lines = TRUE, ref_points = TRUE,                     ref_faces = c("ymax", "xmax")) +       coord_3d()

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: geom_col_3d() produces 3D column charts geom_bar_3d() creates 3D histograms 2D discrete continuous variables geom_voxel_3d() renders sparse 3D pixel data arrays cubes Example: 3D histogram using geom_bar_3d():
code: ggplot(faithful, aes(waiting, eruptions)) +       geom_bar_3d(fill = "steelblue", color = "steelblue",                    bins = 15, width = .9) +       coord_3d(yaw = 60) +       scale_z_continuous(expand = c(0, 0))

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: geom_text_3d() creates 3D-aware text, rendered either “billboard” text faces viewing plane 3D polygons can face direction:
code: df <- expand.grid(x = c("B", "H", "N"), y = c("a", "o", "u"), z = c("g", "t")) df$label <- paste0(df$x, df$y, df$z) ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z, label = label, fill = x)) +       geom_text_3d(method = "polygon", facing = "zmax",                     size = 5, weight = "bold") +       coord_3d(scales = "fixed", rotate_labels = FALSE) +       theme(axis.title = element_blank())

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: Lighting 3D polygon layers controlled providing light() specification layer function coord_3d(). See lighting shading article comprehensive guide.
code: ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +       coord_3d(scales = "fixed") +       scale_fill_viridis_c() +       scale_color_viridis_c() +       theme_dark() +       theme(legend.position = "none") +              # apply shading to solid color/fill       geom_hull_3d(fill = "#8a2900", color = "#8a2900",                    light = light(method = "direct", mode = "hsl",                                   direction = c(0, 0, 1))) +              # apply shading to aesthetic color/fill       geom_hull_3d(aes(x = x + 2.5, fill = x, color = x),                    light = light(method = "diffuse", mode = "hsv",                                   direction = c(0, 0, 1), contrast = 2)) +              # map surface orientation to 3D RGB color channels       geom_hull_3d(aes(x = x + 5),                    light = light(method = "rgb", direction = c(1, 0, -1)))

title: 3D Plotting with ggplot2
text: 3D 2D layers can mixed using position_on_face() project data onto 2D cube faces. saw geom_path_3d() example , ’s another example mixes different geoms, including natively-2D layers like ggplot2::stat_density_2d():
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, Petal.Length,                   color = Species, fill = Species)) +       coord_3d() + xlim(4, 8) +              # place 2D density plot on zmin face       stat_density_2d(position = position_on_face(faces = "zmin", axes = c("x", "y")),                       geom = "polygon", alpha = .1, linewidth = .25) +              # flatten 3D hull layer onto ymax face       geom_hull_3d(position = position_on_face("ymax"), alpha = .5) +              # flatten 3D voxels onto xmax face to create 2D bins       geom_voxel_3d(aes(round(Sepal.Length), round(Sepal.Width), round(Petal.Length)),                     position = position_on_face("xmax"), alpha = .15, light = NULL) +              # 3D scatter plot (added last so it renders in front)       geom_point_3d( shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = .25)

code: # \donttest{ p <- ggplot() +   geom_function_3d(     fun = function(x, y) sin(x) * cos(y),     xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-pi, pi),     fill = "steelblue", color = "steelblue") +   coord_3d()  animate_3d(p, yaw = c(0, 360)) #> Rendering 100 frames... #> Assembling animation... anim_save_3d(filename = file.path(tempdir(), "my_animation.gif")) #> Animation saved to /tmp/RtmpyrkK2W/my_animation.gif # }

title: Animate a 3D plot — animate_3d
text: Renders animated GIF MP4 rotating ggplot coord_3d(), smoothly interpolating rotation angles across frames.

title: Animate a 3D plot — animate_3d
code: animate_3d(   plot,   pitch = NULL,   roll = NULL,   yaw = NULL,   nframes = NULL,   fps = NULL,   duration = NULL,   width = 480,   height = 480,   res = 96,   renderer = gifski_renderer_3d(),   start_pause = 0,   end_pause = 0,   rewind = FALSE,   cores = 1,   device = "png",   progress = interactive() )

title: Animate a 3D plot — animate_3d
text: plot ggplot object uses coord_3d(). pitch, roll, yaw Rotation parameters animate. can : NULL (default): Hold value specified plot's coord_3d() call. single numeric value: Hold value frames (overriding plot's coord). numeric vector length 2+: Keyframe values interpolated linearly across animation. Keyframes evenly spaced, c(0, 180, 180) 100 frames means: start 0, reach 180 frame 50, hold 180 frame 100. nframes, fps, duration Control animation length speed. Specify two three; third computed duration = nframes / fps. Defaults: nframes = 100, fps = 10. width, height Dimensions animation pixels. Defaults 480 x 480. res Resolution ppi rendered frames. Default 96. Controls size point-based text/lines/point elements relative plot. See ?grDevices::png details. renderer renderer function combines image frames animation file. Built-options: gifski_renderer_3d() (default): Renders GIF using gifski package. av_renderer_3d(): Renders video using av package. file_renderer_3d(): Returns frame image files without combining . function signature function(frames, fps) can used. start_pause, end_pause Number frames hold beginning end animation. Default 0. rewind Logical. TRUE, animation plays reverse completing, creating seamless loop. Default FALSE. cores Number CPU cores parallel frame rendering. Default 1 (sequential). Values greater 1 use parallel::parLapply() render frames parallel, can significantly speed animation complex plots. Note progress reporting available parallel rendering. device graphics device use rendering frames. Default "png". options include "ragg_png" (requires ragg package). progress Whether print progress bar. Ignored cores > 1.

title: Animate a 3D plot — animate_3d
text: return value renderer function. gifski_renderer_3d(), gif_3d object (file path class attributes display RStudio knitr). av_renderer_3d(), video_3d object (file path class attributes display). file_renderer_3d(), character vector file paths rendered frame images.

title: Animate a 3D plot — animate_3d
code: # \donttest{ # Plot to animate. Note `anchor = "camera"` keeps light source from rotating. p <- ggplot() +   geom_function_3d(     fun = function(x, y) sin(x) * cos(y),     xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-pi, pi),     fill = "steelblue", color = "steelblue", linewidth = .5) +   coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", anchor = "camera",                          direction = c(1, 1, 0))) +   theme_void()  # Simple turntable rotation # (using small `nframes` to minimize runtime) animate_3d(p, yaw = c(-30, 330), nframes = 12) #> Rendering 12 frames... #> Assembling animation...  # Multi-segment orbit with pitch change animate_3d(p, yaw = c(0, 720), roll = c(-90, 0, -90),            nframes = 12, fps = 15) #> Rendering 12 frames... #> Assembling animation...  # Save to file (writes to a temporary path; specify your own to keep it) anim <- animate_3d(p, yaw = c(0, 360), nframes = 12) #> Rendering 12 frames... #> Assembling animation... anim_save_3d(anim, file.path(tempdir(), "rotating_surface.gif")) #> Animation saved to /tmp/RtmpyrkK2W/rotating_surface.gif # }

title: Compute camera-facing direction for 3D text — camera_facing
text: coord Optional objected created coord_3d. provided, coord parameters drawn pitch, roll, yaw, dist arguments ignored. pitch, roll, yaw Rotation angles degrees, matching values use coord_3d(). dist Distance viewer center data cube, matching value use coord_3d(). Default 2. used mode = "point". mode Character string specifying labels face camera: "plane" (default): labels face parallel view plane, like billboards. typically easier read. "point": label faces toward camera position. Labels edges angle inward, giving perspective-correct orientation.

title: Compute camera-facing direction for 3D text — camera_facing

title: Compute camera-facing direction for 3D text — camera_facing
text: mode = "plane", text labels face direction (parallel viewing plane), similar traditional billboard rendering. produces clean, readable labels. mode = "point", label faces directly toward camera's position 3D space. Labels near edges plot angle inward, geometrically correct can look less clean. Since geom_text_3d() computes vertex positions view known, must specify view parameters use values coord_3d().

title: Compute camera-facing direction for 3D text — camera_facing
code: # \donttest{ # Parallel billboard text (default) - all labels face same direction df <- expand.grid(x = c("H", "B"), y = c("a", "o", "u"), z = c("g", "t")) df$label <- paste0(df$x, df$y, df$z)  ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z = z, label = label)) +   geom_text_3d(method = "polygon",                facing = camera_facing(pitch = 20, roll = -60, yaw = -30)) +   coord_3d(pitch = 20, roll = -60, yaw = -30)   # Point-facing text - labels angle toward camera position ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z = z, label = label)) +   geom_text_3d(method = "polygon",                facing = camera_facing(pitch = 20, roll = -60, yaw = -30,                                       mode = "point")) +   coord_3d(pitch = 20, roll = -60, yaw = -30)  # }

what: Shared column and voxel params — col_params
title: Shared column and voxel params — col_params
text: Shared column voxel params

title: Shared column and voxel params — col_params
text: width Numeric value controlling box width fraction grid spacing. Default 1.0 (volumes touch ). Use 0.8 small gaps, 1.2 overlap. Grid spacing determined automatically using ggplot2::resolution() faces Character vector specifying faces render. Options: "" (default): Render 6 faces "none": Render faces Vector face names: c("zmax", "xmin", "ymax"), etc. Valid face names: "xmin", "xmax", "ymin", "ymax", "zmin", "zmax". Note setting acts jointly backface culling, removes faces whose interior faces viewer – e.g., cull_backfaces = TRUE faces = "" (default), front faces rendered.

title: 3D coordinate system — coord_3d
text: coord_3d 3D coordinate system creates 2D view 3D data. essential core component plot made ggcube. supports rotation, perspective projection, options controlling plot aspect ratios, panel selection, axis label placement, lighting.

title: 3D coordinate system — coord_3d
code: coord_3d(   pitch = 0,   roll = -60,   yaw = -30,   persp = TRUE,   dist = 2,   expand = TRUE,   clip = "off",   panels = "background",   xlabels = "auto",   ylabels = "auto",   zlabels = "auto",   title_position = c("auto", "center"),   rotate_labels = TRUE,   scales = "free",   ratio = c(1, 1, 1),   zoom = 1,   light = ggcube::light(),   ... )

title: 3D coordinate system — coord_3d
text: roll, pitch, yaw Rotation around x, y, z axes, respectively, degrees. Positive values rotate near face plot "downward", "rightward", clockwise, respectively. persp Logical indicating whether apply perspective projection. TRUE (default), objects farther viewer appear smaller. FALSE, produces orthographic projection lines parallel 3D space render parallel plot. dist Distance viewer center data cube. used persp = TRUE. Larger values create less perspective distortion. Default 2. Values less 1 allowed can problematic rendering. expand Logical indicating whether expand axis ranges beyond data range, similar standard ggplot2 behavior. TRUE (default), expansion behavior can controlled using standard axis scaling functions, e.g. ... + scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(.5)). clip Character string indicating clipping behavior. Use "" (default, recommended 3D plots) allow drawing outside plot panel. panels Character vector specifying panels render, including one following: "background" (default), "foreground": faces laying behind front cube's interior volume, respectively. panels vary depending plot rotation. "xmin", "ymax", etc.: names specific cube faces. "", "none": display full cube remove faces. See cube_theming details panel styling, including transparency foreground panels. xlabels, ylabels, zlabels Character strings length-2 character vectors specifying axis label (text title) placement. Labels placed inline grid lines selected panel face. axis, four potential panels labels placed, two potential edges panel. Labels can placed visible faces (see panels argument). parameter accepts: "auto" (default): Automatic edge selection based algorithm prioritizes edges visible periphery plot considers several attributes face geometry better readability. c("face1", "face2"): Manual edge specification using two adjacent face names (e.g., c("xmin", "ymin") selects edge shared xmin ymin faces). first face vector determines face axis labels aligned , second face identifies edge face gets labelled. Available face names : "xmin", "xmax", "ymin", "ymax", "zmin", "zmax". See cube_theming details axis label styling. title_position Character string controlling axis title placement. Currently affects titles internal axes (plot periphery); titles axes plot periphery always centered along axis edge. "auto" (default): Internal axis titles placed near end axis, outside plot area. "center": Internal axis titles centered along axis edge. rotate_labels Logical indicating whether axis labels (text titles) automatically rotate align projected axis directions. FALSE, uses theme text title angle settings. scales Character string specifying aspect ratio behavior: "free" (default): axis scales independently fill cube space, ratio applies standardized coordinates. gives maximum visual range dimension. "fixed": Maintains proportional relationships raw data values, scaled ratio. Similar coord_fixed() 3D (visual ratios match labeled axis ranges). ratio Numeric vector length 3 specifying relative axis lengths c(x, y, z). Defaults c(1, 1, 1) equal proportions. scales = "free": Ratios apply scaled cube coordinates scales = "fixed": Ratios apply original data coordinates zoom Numeric value controlling framing plot. Values greater 1 zoom (tighter framing, may crop edges), values less 1 zoom (whitespace around plot). Default 1. light lighting specification object created light(),"none" disable lighting, NULL inherit plot-level lighting specs coord. Specify plot-level lighting coord_3d() layer-specific lighting geom_*3d() functions. ... Additional arguments reserved internal use.

title: 3D coordinate system — coord_3d
code: # base plot used in examples p <- ggplot() +   geom_function_3d(     aes(fill = after_stat(z), color = after_stat(z)),     fun = function(x, y) sin(x) * cos(y),     xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-pi, pi),     n = 50, light = light("direct", contrast = .7)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   scale_color_viridis_c() +   theme(legend.position = "none")  # 3D plot with default coord settings p + coord_3d()   # \donttest{  # Use `pitch`, `roll`, `yaw` to control plot rotation ----------------------  # zero rotation gives view from x-y face p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 0, yaw = 0)   # pitch rotates plot around y axis p + coord_3d(pitch = 30, roll = 0, yaw = 0)   # roll rotates plot around x axis p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 30, yaw = 0)   # yaw rotates plot around z axis p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = 0, yaw = 30)   # combine them to achieve arbitrary rotations p + coord_3d(pitch = 20, roll = 40, yaw = 60)    # Use `persp` and `dist` to control perspective effects --------------------  # strong perspective effect as if seen from very close p + coord_3d(dist = 1)   # weaker perspective effects as if seen from far away p + coord_3d(dist = 3)   # orthographic projection (`dist = Inf` would be equivalent but it errors) p + coord_3d(persp = FALSE)    # Use `scales` and `ratio` to control aspect ratio -------------------------  # The default "free" scales shown above give cube with maximum visual range. # Use "fixed" scales to make figure match data scales, like coord_fixed. p + coord_3d(scales = "fixed")   # Custom aspect ratios: make y twice as long visually p + coord_3d(scales = "free", ratio = c(1, 2, 1))   # Combine behaviors: fix scales and make y twice as long p + coord_3d(scales = "fixed", ratio = c(1, 2, 1))    # Use `panels` to select which cube faces to render ------------------------  p + coord_3d(panels = c("xmin", "xmax", "zmax"))   # foreground panels default to 20% opaque (can be styled using `theme()`) p + coord_3d(panels = "all")    # Use label params to control axis text placement and rotation -------------  p + coord_3d(xlabels = c("ymax", "zmax"),              zlabels = c("xmax", "ymin"))   p + coord_3d(rotate_labels = FALSE)   # }

title: Using themes to style 3D panels and axis labels — cube_theming
text: ggcube, standard ggplot2 themes generally influence 3D plots expected, including adding complete themes like ggplot2::theme_dark() modifying theme elements like theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill = "darkblue"). text: axis.text.z: Styling z-axis tick labels (inherits axis.text) axis.title.z: Styling z-axis title (inherits axis.title) axis.text, axis.text: Standard styling element_text(). title: Using themes to style 3D panels and axis labels — cube_theming

code: # Basic 3D bar chart from regular grid # (columns extend from z=0 by default) d <- expand.grid(x = 1:5, y = 1:5) d$z <- d$x + d$y + rnorm(25, 0, 0.5) ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_col_3d() +   coord_3d()   # Set uniform base level using `zmin` parameter ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_col_3d(aes(fill = z), color = "white",               zmin = 5) +   coord_3d()   # Set variable base levels using `zmin` aesthetic d$base_level <- runif(nrow(d), -5, 1) ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z = z, zmin = base_level)) +   geom_col_3d(color = "black") +   coord_3d()   # Show only a subset of column faces ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_col_3d(faces = c("zmax", "ymin"),     cull_backfaces = FALSE,     fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +   coord_3d()   # With gaps between columns ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_col_3d(color = "black", width = 0.6) +   coord_3d()

title: 3D surface from smoothed conditional means — geom_smooth_3d
code: # Generate scattered 3D data set.seed(123) d <- data.frame(   x = runif(100, -1, 3),   y = runif(100, -3, 3) ) d$z <- abs(1 + d$x^2 - d$y^2 + rnorm(100, 0, 1))  # Base plot p <- ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   coord_3d(light = NULL) +   scale_fill_viridis_c()  # Basic smooth surface with default loess model p + geom_smooth_3d()   # \donttest{  # Show data points p + geom_smooth_3d(points = TRUE)   # Show data points with residual lines p + geom_smooth_3d(points = TRUE, residuals = TRUE,       point_color = "red", alpha = .8)   # Linear model surface with 90% confidence intervals p + geom_smooth_3d(aes(fill = after_stat(level)),       method = "lm", color = "black", se = TRUE,       level = 0.99, se_alpha = .7, n = 10) +       scale_fill_manual(values = c("red", "darkorchid4", "steelblue")) #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.   # Linear model surface with custom model formula p + geom_smooth_3d(method = "lm", n = 10,       formula = z ~ poly(x, 2) + poly(y, 2) + x:y)   # Loess with custom span parameter, and lighting effects p + geom_smooth_3d(       method = "loess", method.args = list(span = 0.3),       fill = "steelblue", color = "white", n = 20,       light = light(direction = c(0, -1, 0), color = FALSE))   # GLM with gamma family and log link p + geom_smooth_3d(       method = "glm", n = 10,       method.args = list(family = Gamma(link = "log")),       formula = z ~ poly(x, 2) + poly(y, 2))   # Visualize uncertainty with computed "standard error" variable p + geom_smooth_3d(aes(fill = after_stat(se * 2))) +   scale_fill_viridis_c() #> Scale for fill is already present. #> Adding another scale for fill, which will replace the existing scale.   # Extend surface beyond training data range (explicit extrapolation) p + geom_smooth_3d(method = "lm", xlim = c(-5, 5), ylim = c(-5, 5))   # Clip surface to predictor convex hull # to prevent extrapolation into corner areas p + geom_smooth_3d(method = "lm", domain = "chull")   # Specify alternative grid geometry p + geom_smooth_3d(grid = "right1", n = 30, direction = "y")   # Separate fits for data subgroups ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, qsec, fill = factor(cyl))) +   geom_smooth_3d(method = "lm", alpha = .7,     xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(0, 40)) + # specify shared domain   coord_3d() + theme_light()   # }

code: df <- expand.grid(x = c("B", "H", "N"), y = c("a", "o", "u"), z = c("g", "t")) df$label <- paste0(df$x, df$y, df$z)  # Billboard text (default) - automatically faces camera ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z, label = label)) +   geom_text_3d() +   coord_3d(scales = "fixed")   # Polygon text - can face any direction ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z, label = label, fill = z)) +   geom_text_3d(method = "polygon", facing = "zmax") +   coord_3d(scales = "fixed")   # Polygon text facing camera (using coord parameter) my_coord <- coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -60, yaw = -30, scales = "fixed") ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z, label = label)) +   geom_text_3d(method = "polygon", facing = "camera", coord = my_coord,                color = "red", linewidth = .1) +   my_coord   # Text with styling ggplot(df, aes(x, y, z, label = label, fill = factor(x))) +   geom_text_3d(method = "polygon", facing = "xmin",                family = "serif", weight = "bold", italic = TRUE,                size = 6, aspect_adjust = 2) +   coord_3d(scales = "fixed")

title: Grid generation — grid_generation
text: grid Character specifying tile geometry. Options: "rectangle" Rectangular grid (default). "right1" Rectangular grid quad split two right triangles along bottom-left top-right diagonal. "right2" Like "right1" split along bottom-right top-left diagonal. "equilateral" Equilateral triangular lattice (tessellated via Delaunay triangulation). Can prevent lighting artifacts surface curves past parallel sight line. n Either single integer specifying grid resolution dimensions, vector length 2 specifying c(nx, ny) separate x y resolutions. Default 40. Higher values create smoother surfaces slower rendering. direction Either "x" (default) "y", specifying orientation tile rows. Ignored rectangular grids. trim Logical. relevant grid = "equilateral". TRUE (default), trims edge vertices grid boundaries straight lines. FALSE, preserves full lattice, resulting grid irregular edges.

what: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light
title: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light
text: Creates lighting specification object use 3D polygon layers. Lighting modifies brightness fill /base color aesthetics based surface orientation (.e., implements form shadows cast shadows). Various options available control light qualities light source location. result can passed coord_3d() application whole plot, individual geom_*_3d() layer functions (take precedence plot-level lighting).

title: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light
code: light(   method = "diffuse",   direction = c(-0.5, 0, 1),   anchor = "scene",   position = NULL,   distance_falloff = FALSE,   fill = TRUE,   color = TRUE,   mode = "hsv",   contrast = 1,   backface_scale = -1,   backface_offset = 0 )

title: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light
text: method Character string specifying lighting model: "diffuse": default. Atmospheric lighting soft shadows (surfaces pointing directly away light source fully dark; base color occurs surfaces perpendicular light) "direct": Direct lighting hard shadows (surfaces angled beyond 90 degrees light source fully dark; base color occurs surfaces angled 45 degrees toward light) "rgb": Map surface orientation three dimensional color space. Rather light model darkening brightening base colors models, model generates wholly new set colors. option selected, parameters like mode contrast ignored, fill, color, direction still apply. direction Numeric vector length 3 specifying direction 3D space (x, y, z) light comes directional lighting. Depending anchor, direction relative either plot axes viewing pane. default c(-.5, 0, 1), giving diagonal lighting upper right edge default rotation anchor. least one value must non-zero. Values automatically normalized, magnitude matter, sign relative magnitude. Direction specified visual space (relative rendered cube's bounding box), data space. argument ignored position provided. anchor Character string specifying reference frame light direction: "scene" (default): Light direction fixed relative data/scene. Light rotates plot, regardless rotation, direction = c(1, 0, 0) lights surfaces facing "xmax" cube face, example. "camera": Light direction fixed relative camera/viewer. Light direction stays place regardless rotation, direction = c(1, 0, 0) lights surfaces facing right side plot, example. rotation parameters zero, options give result. position Numeric vector length 3 specifying light source position data coordinate space positional lighting. specified, face gets light direction calculated light position face center. Mutually exclusive direction. Default NULL (use directional lighting). distance_falloff Logical indicating whether apply distance-based intensity falloff positional lighting using inverse square law (intensity proportional 1/distance²). used position specified. Default FALSE. fill Logical indicating whether apply lighting fill colors. Default TRUE. color Logical indicating whether apply lighting border/line colors. Default TRUE. mode Character string specifying color lighting mode: "hsv": default. Modifies value component HSV color (fades bright colors high end, black low end). "hsl": Modifies lightness component HSL color (fades white high end, black low end). two options give identical results grayscale colors. contrast Numeric value greater zero controlling intensity lighting effects. 1.0 (default) gives full black--white range. Values less 1 give subtler effects, values greater 1 give dramatic effects. backface_scale, backface_offset Numeric values determine "frontface" light values get modified (scaled offset) derive "backface" light values. backface side polygon faces underside surface inside volume. Frontface light values typically range [-1, 1] (unless contrast boosted). default scale -1 gives backfaces highly contrasting lighting frontfaces. light backfaces frontfaces, set scale 1. uniformly darken (brighten) backfaces, use negative (positive) offset.

title: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light

title: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light
text: Note light-like effects can also achieved stats mapping color aesthestics computed variables after_stat(dzdx); see geom_surface_3d() examples.

title: Lighting specification for 3D surface rendering — light
code: # base plot used in examples p <- ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_hull_3d(fill = "#9e2602", color = "#5e1600")  # Light qualities ----------------------------------------------------------  # default `"diffuse"` lighting p + coord_3d()   # \donttest{  # use `"direct"` lighting to apply full shade to unlit surfaces p + coord_3d(light = light(method = "direct"))   # use `"hsl"` mode to fade highlights to white p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl"))   # adjust lighting intensity with `contrast` p + coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", contrast = 1.5))   # use `"rgb"` lighting to map face orientation to 3D color space p + coord_3d(light = light(method = "rgb"))    # Lighting targets ---------------------------------------------------------  # use `fill` and `color` to select which aesthetics get modified by light p + coord_3d(light = light(fill = TRUE, color = FALSE))   # apply lighting to aesthetically mapped colors, with shaded guide to match p + geom_hull_3d(aes(fill = x, color = x)) +       scale_fill_viridis_c() +       scale_color_viridis_c() +       guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d()) +       coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", contrast = .9))   # disable lighting entirely # (equivalent to specifying `light(fill = FALSE, color = FALSE`)) p + coord_3d(light = "none")   # if provided, layer-level lighting overrides plot-level (coord_3d) lighting p + coord_3d(light = light("direct"), # plot-level: affects original layer       scales = "fixed") +   geom_hull_3d(aes(x = x + 2.5), fill = "#9e2602", color = "#5e1600",       light = light("direct", mode = "hsl", direction = c(0, -1, 0)))    # Light sources ------------------------------------------------------------  # directional light from the xmin-ymin-zmin direction # (`direction` is relative to rotated axes with default `anchor = "scene"`) p + coord_3d(light = light(direction = c(-1, -1, -1)))   # directional light from top-right corner of figure # (`anchor = "camera"` makes `direction` fixed relative to the plot) p + coord_3d(light = light(direction = c(1, 1, 0), anchor = "camera"))   # positional light source within plot ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +   stat_surface_3d(fill = "red", color = "red") +   coord_3d(     light = light(position = c(.5, .7, 95), distance_falloff = TRUE,                   mode = "hsl", contrast = .9),     ratio = c(1.5, 2, 1))    # Backface lighting --------------------------------------------------------  # backfaces get "opposite" lighting by default (`backface_scale = -1`) p <- ggplot() +   geom_function_3d(fun = function(x, y) x^2 + y^2,     xlim = c(-3, 3), ylim = c(-3, 3),     fill = "steelblue", color = "steelblue") p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -70, yaw = 0,              light = light(mode = "hsl"))   # use `backface_scale = 1` to light backfaces as if they're frontfaces p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -70, yaw = 0,              light = light(backface_scale = 1, mode = "hsl"))   # use `backface_offset` to uniformly darken (or lighten) backfaces p + coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -70, yaw = 0,              light = light(backface_scale = 1, mode = "hsl",                            backface_offset = -.5))   # }

title: Position for projecting 2D and 3D layers onto cube faces — position_on_face
code: # 3D point layer in raw 3D form, and projected onto 2D face ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +   geom_point_3d(position = position_on_face("zmin"), color = "red") +   geom_point_3d(color = "black") + # add this layer last so it appears on top   coord_3d()   # 3D layer projected to multiple faces set.seed(1) d <- data.frame(x = round(rnorm(10)), y = round(rnorm(10)), z = round(rnorm(10))) ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +   stat_voxel_3d(color = "black", fill = "steelblue",     light = light(direction = c(1, 1, 0), mode = "hsv"),     position = position_on_face(c("3D", "zmin", "xmax", "ymax"))) +   coord_3d()   # 3D layer projected differently on individual faces ggplot(sphere_points, aes(x, y, z)) +   stat_hull_3d(position = position_on_face("zmin"), fill = "black") +   geom_point_3d(position = position_on_face("ymax")) +   geom_path_3d(position = position_on_face("xmax")) +   stat_hull_3d(color = "black") +   coord_3d()   # 2D density contour on a specific face ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, Petal.Length, color = Species)) +   stat_density_2d(position = position_on_face(faces = "zmin", axes = c("x", "y"))) +   geom_point_3d() +   coord_3d() #> Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: z. #> ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in #>   the data. #> ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical #>   variable into a factor?   # Distinct 2D layers projected to different faces ggplot(mtcars) +   geom_smooth(aes(mpg, qsec), color = "red", alpha = .5, se = FALSE,     position = position_on_face(faces = "ymax", axes = c("x", "z"))) +   geom_density_2d(aes(mpg, wt), alpha = .5,     position = position_on_face(faces = "zmin", axes = c("x", "y"))) +   geom_path(aes(wt, qsec), color = "forestgreen", alpha = .5,     position = position_on_face(faces = "xmax", axes = c("y", "z"))) +   geom_point_3d(aes(mpg, wt, qsec)) +     coord_3d() +   theme_light() #> `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Depth sorting method — sorting_methods
text: Depth sorting method

title: Depth sorting method — sorting_methods
text: sort_method Character indicating algorithm used determine order elements rendered. controls depth sorting geometry types within layer, including polygons, points, segments, text. Default varies geometry type. "painter": Elements sorted mean depth (distance viewer rotation) vertices. fast, can give incorrect results primitives overlap screen space different depths. "pairwise": intensive sorting algorithm compares every pair elements determine occlusion order. Uses type-specific geometric tests: polygon overlap detection polygon-polygon pairs, point--polygon tests polygon-point pairs, line clipping polygon-segment pairs, line intersection segment-segment pairs. elements coplanar, smaller primitives (points, segments) render top larger ones (polygons). Slower accurate. "auto": Uses pairwise data fewer 500 rows, painter otherwise.

Must contain x y columns point coordinates. stat statistical transformation use data. Defaults StatDensity3D. position Position adjustment, defaults "identity". collapse result onto one 2D surface, use position_on_face(). ... arguments passed layer function (typically GeomPolygon3D), aesthetics like colour, fill, linewidth, annotate = annotate_3d(...), etc. grid, n, direction, trim Parameters determining geometry, resolution, orientation surface grid. See grid_generation details. h Bandwidth vector. NULL (default), uses automatic bandwidth selection via MASS::bandwidth.nrd(). Can single number (used dimensions) vector length 2 different bandwidths x y directions. adjust Multiplicative bandwidth adjustment factor. Values greater 1 produce smoother surfaces; values less 1 produce detailed surfaces. Default 1. pad Proportional range expansion factor. computed density grid extends proportion raw data range beyond data limit. Default 0.1. min_ndensity Lower cutoff normalized density (computed variable ndensity described ), filter results. particularly useful removing low-density corners rectangular density grids density surfaces shown multiple groups, example . Default 0 (filtering). light lighting specification object created light(),"none" disable lighting, NULL inherit plot-level lighting specs coord. Specify plot-level lighting coord_3d() layer-specific lighting geom_*3d() functions. cull_backfaces Logical indicating whether remove back-facing polygons rendering. primarily performance optimization may useful aesthetic reasons situations. Backfaces determined using screen-space winding order 3D transformation. code: # Basic density surface from scattered points p <- ggplot(faithful, aes(eruptions, waiting)) +   coord_3d() +   scale_fill_viridis_c() p + geom_density_3d() + guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d())   # \donttest{  # Specify alternative grid geometry and light model p + geom_density_3d(grid = "equilateral", n = 30, direction = "y",                     light = light("direct"),                     color = "white", linewidth = .1) +   guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d())   # Color by alternative density metric p + geom_density_3d(aes(fill = after_stat(count)))   # Adjust bandwidth for smoother or more detailed surfaces p + geom_density_3d(adjust = 0.5, n = 100, color = "white")  # More detail  p + geom_density_3d(adjust = 2, color = "white")   # Smoother   # As contour plot instead of default surface plot p + stat_density_3d(geom = "contour_3d", light = "none",                     color = "black", bins = 25,                     sort_method = "pairwise")   # Multiple density surfaces by group, # using normalized density to equalize peak heights ggplot(iris, aes(Petal.Length, Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +   geom_density_3d(aes(z = after_stat(ndensity), group = Species),                   color = "black", alpha = .7, light = NULL) +   coord_3d()   # Same, but with extra padding to remove edge effects and # with density filtering to remove rectangular artifacts ggplot(iris, aes(Petal.Length, Sepal.Length, fill = Species)) +   geom_density_3d(aes(z = after_stat(ndensity)),                   pad = .3, min_ndensity = .001,                   color = "black", alpha = .7, light = NULL) +   coord_3d(ratio = c(3, 3, 1))   # }

Defaults geom_ridgeline_3d(). position Position adjustment, defaults "identity". collapse result onto one 2D surface, use position_on_face(). ... arguments passed layer. direction Direction ridges: "x" One ridge per unique x value; ridge varies y (default) "y" One ridge per unique y value; ridge varies x bw smoothing bandwidth used. numeric, standard deviation smoothing kernel. character, rule choose bandwidth, listed stats::bw.nrd(). Options include "nrd0" (default), "nrd", "ucv", "bcv", "SJ", "SJ-ste", "SJ-dpi". Note automatic calculation performed per-group unless joint_bandwidth = TRUE. adjust multiplicative bandwidth adjustment. makes possible adjust bandwidth still using bandwidth estimator. example, adjust = 1/2 means use half default bandwidth. Default 1. kernel Kernel function use. One "gaussian" (default), "rectangular", "triangular", "epanechnikov", "biweight", "cosine", "optcosine". See stats::density() details. n Number equally spaced points density estimated. power two efficiency. Default 512. trim FALSE (default), density computed full range data (extended factor based bandwidth). TRUE, density computed range group . bounds numeric vector length 2 giving lower upper bounds bounded density estimation. Density values outside bounds set zero. Data points outside bounds removed warning. Default c(-Inf, Inf) (unbounded). rel_min_height Lines heights cutoff removed. cutoff measured relative maximum height within group. example, rel_min_height = 0.01 removes points density less 1% peak. Default 0 (removal). similar parameter name ggridges::geom_density_ridges(). joint_bandwidth TRUE, bandwidth computed jointly across groups using specified bw method, ensuring consistent smoothing across density curves. matches behavior ggridges::stat_density_ridges(). FALSE (default), bandwidth computed separately group, matching ggplot2::stat_density(). applies bw character string (bandwidth rule), bw provided numeric value. base Z-value ridge polygon bottoms. Scaling based mean depth polygon. force_convex Logical indicating whether remove polygon vertices part convex hull. Default value varies geom. text: direction parameter determines data interpreted: direction = NULL (default) Automatically detects direction based whether x y discrete (factor/character). x discrete y continuous, uses "x"; y discrete x continuous, uses "y". Falls back "x" ambiguous. direction = "x" Ridges march along x-axis. unique x value defines group, density computed y values within group. resulting density curves lie y-z plane. direction = "y" Ridges march along y-axis. unique y value defines group, density computed x values. resulting density curves lie x-z plane.

code: library(ggplot2)  # Basic usage with iris data p <- ggplot(iris, aes(y = Sepal.Length, x = Species, fill = Species)) +   coord_3d() +   scale_z_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, NA))) + # remove gap beneath ridges   theme(legend.position = "none") p + stat_distributions_3d()   # Normalize max ridge heights p + stat_distributions_3d(aes(z = after_stat(ndensity)))   # Adjust smoothing bandwidth p + stat_distributions_3d(adjust = 0.5)   # Use joint bandwidth for consistent smoothing across groups p + stat_distributions_3d(joint_bandwidth = TRUE) #> Picking joint bandwidth of 0.274   # Different bandwidth selection rules p + stat_distributions_3d(bw = "SJ")   # Remove tails with rel_min_height p + stat_distributions_3d(rel_min_height = 0.05)   # Trim to data range p + stat_distributions_3d(trim = TRUE)   # Rotated to reduce perspective distortion p + stat_distributions_3d(alpha = .7) +    coord_3d(pitch = 0, roll = -90, yaw = 90, dist = 5,       panels = c("zmin", "xmin")) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.

title: 3D surface from point data — stat_surface_3d
text: stat automatically detects whether data forms regular grid checking nrow(data) == length(unique(x)) * length(unique(y)). Regular grids get point-level gradient computation; irregular point clouds passed Delaunay tessellation geom.

title: 3D surface from point data — stat_surface_3d
code: # Regular grid data ------------------------------------------  # simulated data and base plot for basic surface d <- dplyr::mutate(tidyr::expand_grid(x = -10:10, y = -10:10),                    z = sqrt(x^2 + y^2) / 1.5,                    z = cos(z) - z) p <- ggplot(d, aes(x, y, z)) +       coord_3d(light = light(mode = "hsl", direction = c(1, 0, 0)))  # surface with 3d lighting p + geom_surface_3d(fill = "steelblue", color = "steelblue", linewidth = .2)   # \donttest{  # mesh wireframe (`fill = NA`) with aes line color p + geom_surface_3d(aes(color = z), fill = NA,                     linewidth = .5, light = "none") +       scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("black", "blue", "red"))   # triangulated surface (can prevent lighting flaws) p + geom_surface_3d(fill = "#9e2602", color = "black", grid = "right2")   # use after_stat to access computed surface-orientation variables p + geom_surface_3d(aes(fill = after_stat(slope)), grid = "right2") +       scale_fill_viridis_c() +       guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d())   # use `group` to plot data for multiple surfaces d <- expand.grid(x = -5:5, y = -5:5) d$z <- d$x^2 - d$y^2 d$g <- "a" d2 <- d d2$z <- d$z + 15 d2$g <- "b" ggplot(rbind(d, d2), aes(x, y, z, group = g, fill = g)) +       coord_3d(light = "none") +       geom_surface_3d(color = "black", alpha = .5, light = NULL)   # terrain surface with topographic hillshade and elevational fill ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z, fill = z, color = z)) +       geom_surface_3d(light = light(direction = c(1, 0, .5),                                     mode = "hsv", contrast = 1.5),                       linewidth = .2) +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1, 1.5, .75)) +       theme_light() +       scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("darkgreen", "rosybrown4", "gray60")) +       scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("darkgreen", "rosybrown4", "gray60")) +       guides(fill = guide_colorbar_3d())   # stack of flat surfaces (e.g. a time series of raster maps) d <- expand.grid(lon = 1:20, lat = 1:20, time = 1:6) d$value <- sin(-d$lon * d$lat / 10) + d$time / 2 ggplot(d, aes(lon, lat, time, group = time,               fill = value, color = value)) +    geom_surface_3d(light = "none") +    coord_3d() +    scale_color_viridis_c() + scale_fill_viridis_c()   # stat_surface_3d with alternative geoms ----------------------------  # horizontal slices with geom_ridgeline_3d ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z)) +   stat_surface_3d(geom = "ridgeline_3d",                   fill = "black", color = "white",                   light = "none", linewidth = .1) +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1, 1.5, .75), yaw = 45)   # elevation contours with geom_contour_3d ggplot(mountain, aes(x, y, z, fill = z)) +   stat_surface_3d(geom = "contour_3d", light = "none",                   bins = 25, sort_method = "pairwise",                   color = "black") +       coord_3d(ratio = c(1, 1.5, .75), yaw = 45) +       scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B")    # Irregular point data ---------------------------------------  set.seed(42) pts <- data.frame(x = runif(200, -2, 2), y = runif(200, -2, 2)) pts$z <- with(pts, sin(x) * cos(y))  ggplot(pts, aes(x, y, z = z, fill = z)) +   stat_surface_3d(sort_method = "pairwise") +   scale_fill_viridis_c() +   coord_3d(light = "none")   # }
